revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東求女塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kobe|qid=Q48320|1=Kobe}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.712927777778|N|135.26409444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}
[[File:Higashimotomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px|thumb|Remaining mound of the Higashi-Motomezuka Kofun]] '''Higashi-Motomezuka Kofun''' (東求女塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, or keyhole-shaped Kofun, located in {{ill|Higashinada-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市東灘区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinada-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan. It is not designated as a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}.
It is said to be the tomb of '''Chinu Otoko (茅渟壮士)''', a figure from the '''legend of Ubara no Otome (菟原処女の伝説)'''. However, in reality, it is believed to be the tomb of a '''local ruling family (豪族)'''.
== Overview == {{maplink2 |display= |frame=yes |title=Ancient Kofun in Higashinada-ku |description= |frame-latitude=34.7111875 |frame-longitude=135.2474375 |frame-width=280 |frame-height=250 |zoom=12
|type=point |coord={{Coord|34|42|18.38|N|135|13|35.41|E}} |marker=1
|type2=point |coord2={{Coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}} |marker2=2
|type3=point |coord3={{Coord|34|42|46.54|N|135|15|50.74|E}} |marker3=3
|text=1. [[Nishi-Motomezuka Kofun]] 2. [[Shojotsuka Kofun]] 3. Higashi-Motomezuka Kofun}}
[[File:Higashimotomezuka Kofun, sekihi.jpg|thumb|280px|right|{{center|Monument of Higashi-Motomezuka}}]]
Since the site was originally '''privately owned''', the mound has been '''significantly altered''' and does not retain its original shape. Today, the western part (the front section) is occupied by '''{{ill|Kobe Municipal Yuki Kindergarten|ja|神戸市立遊喜幼稚園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Municipal Yuki Kindergarten|1=Kobe Municipal Yuki Kindergarten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590096}}''', while the eastern part (the round rear section) has been '''converted into Motomezuka East Park'''. The remaining circular mound has been '''reinforced with rocks''', and a '''monument inscribed with "東求女塚之碑" (Higashi-Motomezuka Monument)''' has been placed on top.
=== '''Original Dimensions and Excavations''' === According to the '''1883 (Meiji 16) publication "Sumiyoshi Village Gazetteer" (住吉村地誌)''', the kofun originally had: * '''Height:''' Approx. 3m (一丈) * '''Circumference:''' Approx. 154m (八四七間) * '''Estimated full length:''' '''80 meters''' * '''Front section:''' '''Width 42m × Length 42m''' * '''Rear circular section:''' '''Diameter 47m'''
During an '''1872 (Meiji 5) excavation''', archaeologists discovered: * '''Six bronze mirrors (銅鏡)''' – '''Four of which were''' {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} * '''Magatama (勾玉)''' * '''Tachi swords (大刀)''' * '''Wheel-shaped stone artifacts (車輪石) made of jasper (碧玉)'''
A '''1982 (Showa 57) excavation''' of the '''northwestern front section''' uncovered: * '''Fukiishi (葺石)''' * '''Moat (周溝)''' * '''Sue pottery (須恵器) and Haji pottery (土師器)''' However, '''no haniwa (埴輪)''' were found.{{Sfn|Kobe City Board of Education|1987|pp=23-42}}
== History == Because the land was privately owned, it '''changed hands multiple times''', leading to further destruction of the mound: * Some of the '''mound soil was removed''' and used for '''farming and house walls'''. * During the construction of the '''Hanshin Electric Railway (阪神電鉄)''', soil was excavated to '''create an embankment''' for crossing the '''{{ill|Sumiyoshi River (Hyōgo Prefecture)|ja|住吉川 (兵庫県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi River (Hyōgo Prefecture)|1=Sumiyoshi River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11381826}}'''.
As a result, '''concerns arose among local residents''', and the '''village council (村会協議会)''' decided to intervene. In '''1904 (Meiji 37)''', the village '''exchanged the land''' for another municipal property to preserve it.
* The '''western front section''' became '''a flower garden''' (花六). * The '''eastern rear section''' became a '''waste disposal site'''.
In '''1915 (Taisho 4)''', local '''Sumiyoshi Village''' residents worked together to '''restore the site''', reinforcing it with '''stone walls''' and planting '''plum, pine, and maple trees'''. A '''monument''' was also erected to commemorate the kofun. Later, in '''1918 (Taisho 7)''', '''{{ill|Fusanosuke Kuhara|en|Fusanosuke Kuhara|qid=Q5509880|lt=Hisanojo Kuhara|1=Fusanosuke Kuhara}}''' established '''Yuki Kindergarten (遊喜園)''', which preserved the eastern part of the kofun.
{{Cquote|<center>'''Monument Inscription: "求女塚之碑" (Motomezuka Monument)'''</center> Pigeons cannot forget the Yamato trees; Shigi birds cannot forget the marshlands; Likewise, our people cannot step outside history. Thus, preserving ancient sites is not just a matter of '''protecting tradition'''— It is a way to '''cultivate a strong sense of patriotism''' and inspire future generations. Let us preserve this historic site for those who come after us.
*Written by Instructor Morikawa (訓導森川選書)* '''November, Taisho 4 (1915)'''}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|year=1946|title=住吉村誌|publisher={{ill|Mukogun|ja|武庫郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukogun|1=Muko district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11545988}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Village (Hyōgo)|ja|住吉村 (兵庫県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Village (Hyōgo)|1=Sumiyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18700218}}}} *{{Cite book|和書|author={{ill|Kobe City Board of Education|ja|神戸市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Board of Education|1=Kobe City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11589927}}|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/11073?all=%E6%9D%B1%E6%B1%82%E5%A5%B3%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3l|chapter=Excavation Report of Higashi-Motomezuka Kofun (1982)|title=Showa 57 Archaeological Reports|publisher={{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|qid=Q48320|lt=Kobe City|1=Kobe}}|year=1987|date=1987-10-25|pages=23-42|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|Michitani Taku|ja|道谷卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michitani Taku|1=Takashi Michitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11641809}}|year=1989|title=Higashinada in Japanese History|publisher={{ill|Kobe City Culture Promotion Foundation|ja|神戸市民文化振興財団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Culture Promotion Foundation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kobe City Higashinada Cultural Center}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Higashimotomezuka Kofun}} * [[Nishi-Motomezuka Kofun]] – Believed to be the tomb of Ubara Otoko (菟原壮士) * [[Shojotsuka Kofun]] – Believed to be the tomb of Ubara no Otome (菟原処女)
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Kofun !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Higashi Tomezuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tomezuka Kofun|qq=}}|| Hyōgo Prefecture, Kobe, Higashinada Ward || Keyhole-shaped mound || Early Period || Four mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Koyadani Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyadani Kofun|qq=}} || Hyōgo Prefecture, {{ill|Sumoto, Hyōgo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumoto, Hyōgo|qq=}}|| || || |- | {{ill|Yoshijima Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshijima Kofun|qq=}} || Hyōgo Prefecture, {{ill|Tatsuno, Hyōgo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuno, Hyōgo|qq=}}|| Keyhole-shaped mound || || Four mirrors excavated out of six; same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound |- | {{ill|Morio Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morio Kofun|qq=}} || Hyōgo Prefecture, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyooka, Hyōgo|qq=}}, Morio, Ichio|| || || Mirror inscribed "Zhengshi First Year" |- ! colspan=5| Chūgoku Region |- | {{ill|Bizen Kurazuka Kofun|ja|備前車塚古墳|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kurazuka Kofun|qq=}} || Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}|| Keyhole-shaped mound || || 11 mirrors excavated out of 13 |- | {{ill|Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun|lt=Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun|lt=Tsuruoyama Maruyama Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruoyama Maruyama Kofun|qq=}} || Okayama Prefecture, {{ill|Bizen, Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen, Okayama|qq=}}|| Round mound || || One mirror excavated out of 30 |- | {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun (disambiguation)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun|qq=}} || Hiroshima Prefecture, {{ill|Higashihiroshima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashihiroshima|qq=}}, Takaya Town|| || || |- | {{ill|Shiozaki Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiozaki Kofun|qq=}} || Hiroshima Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuyama|qq=}}, Shinichi Town|| || || |- | {{ill|Nakakoda No. 1 Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakakoda No. 1 Kofun|qq=}} || Hiroshima Prefecture, {{ill|Hiroshima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima|qq=}}, Higashi Ward|| || || Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound |- | {{ill|Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kanbara Shrine|lt=Kambara Shrine Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kambara Shrine Kofun|qq=}} || Shimane Prefecture, {{ill|Unnan, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unnan, Shimane|qq=}}, Kamo Town|| || || Mirror inscribed "Jingchu Third Year" |- | {{ill|Takeshima Go-Karō Residence Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshima Go-Karō Residence Kofun|qq=}} || Yamaguchi Prefecture, {{ill|Shūnan, Yamaguchi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūnan, Yamaguchi|qq=}}|| || || Mirror inscribed "Zhengshi First Year" |- ! colspan=5| Shikoku Region |- | {{ill|Oku No. 3 Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oku No. 3 Kofun|qq=}} || Kagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki, Kagawa|qq=}}, Samukawa Town|| Keyhole-shaped mound || Early Kofun Period || |- | {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} || Kagawa Prefecture, Sanuki, Kamobe Nishiyama || Keyhole-shaped mound || Early Kofun Period || |- | {{ill|Miyatani Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyatani Kofun|qq=}}|| Tokushima Prefecture, {{ill|Tokushima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima|qq=}}, Kokufu Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd to early 4th century || |- ! colspan=5| Kyushu Region |- | {{ill|Naka Hachiman Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Hachiman Kofun|qq=}}|| Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka|qq=}}, Hakata Ward || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-3rd century || Five-God Four-Beast Mirror |- | {{ill|Ishizukayama Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}}|| Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Miyako District, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako District, Fukuoka|qq=}} , {{ill|Kanda, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda, Fukuoka|qq=}}|| Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd century || Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound; seven mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Ikisan Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Ikisan Chōshizuka Kofun|qid=Q48752705|lt=Ikisan Chōshizuka Kofun|1=Ikisan Chōshizuka Kofun}} || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoshima, Fukuoka|qq=}}|| Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || Eight locally produced mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Iwagami Ritual Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwagami Ritual Site|qq=}} || Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Okinoshima|| || Late 4th to early 5th century || Ancient ritual site, not a Kofun |- | {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|lt=Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|lt=Akasuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasuka Kofun|qq=}} || Ōita Prefecture, {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa, Ōita|qq=}}, Usa Fudoki Hill|| Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 3rd century || Part of {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|qq=}}; Kyushu's oldest Kofun; five mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Mochida Kofun Cluster|lt=Mochida Kofun Cluster|lt=Mochida Kofuns|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mochida Kofuns|qq=}}|| Miyazaki Prefecture, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyu District, Miyazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Takanabe, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takanabe, Miyazaki|qq=}}, Mochida || || || Mirror inscribed "Heavenly King Sun and Moon." Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound<ref name="miyazaki">{{Cite journal |和書 |author=Noriyuki Imashioya |year=2010 |title=The Formation and Evaluation of the Mochida Kofuns in Takanabe Town, Miyazaki Prefecture |journal=Miyazaki Cultural Studies Research Bulletin |issue=37 |publisher=Miyazaki Prefectural Library |pages=47-65 |crid=1522543655217461888 |url=https://www.lib.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/ct/other000001600/kiyouimashioya.pdf]]</ref><ref>{{Cite Kotobank |word=Mochida Kofuns |encyclopedia=Nipponica Encyclopedia |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref> |- | Mochida Old Mound No. 48 || Miyazaki Prefecture, Koyu District, Takanabe Town, Mochida || Round mound || || Part of Mochida Kofuns. Same mold as Maebashi Tenjinyama Mound<ref name="miyazaki" />. Extensively looted in the early Shōwa period<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1849|title=Cultural Properties of Miyazaki: Mochida Kofuns |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref>. |}
